headline: For Oman, the Strait of Hormuz is a windfall and a burden
description: The monarchy, which draws its influence from its geographic position on the edge of the maritime corridor, has been unsettled by Iran's strategy of monetizing the passage. With its hands tied by a longstanding tradition of mediation and neutrality, Muscat has struggled to assert itself.

headline: After a foiled bombing outside Bank of America in Paris, five arrests and questions over Iran's role
Ireland gives Trump a lesson on Europe
Trump threatens to destroy Kharg Island, Iran's oil export hub
description: The US president said he would destroy the island, which houses oil wells, power plants, and other civilian infrastructure, if a deal is not soon reached to end the war.

headline: G7 ministers call for stabilizing energy markets amid Middle East war
description: Ministers of the group of industrial powers said they are prepared to take 'all necessary measures' to stabilize the global energy market, which is facing disruptions with Middle Eastern oil shipments.

headline: Celine Dion announces return to Paris stage this September and October
description: The 58-year-old Canadian star had been on a long hiatus after a health condition, but announced on Monday that she was ready to return to the French capital for 10 shows.

headline: Assault, attempted murder, assassination: The rogue Freemasons going on trial in Paris
description: A coach, a race car driver, a trade unionist: Nothing seemed to connect these otherwise unremarkable individuals. Yet all were targeted by the same criminal organization. At its head were prominent members of a Masonic lodge in a Paris suburb. These 'brothers,' now enemies, are standing trial from March 30.

headline: Air Canada CEO announces retirement after row over English-only condolence message

UN force in Lebanon says soldiers killed in country's south

Several new far-right mayors remove EU flags from French town halls
Christian Jambet, Islamic scholar: 'The true leaders of Iran are now soldiers who want to spread the Islamic Revolution beyond Iran's border'
description: The philosopher and member of the Académie Française, an expert on Shiite spiritualities, shares his thoughts on the ongoing war and the 'degeneration' of the Iranian regime.

headline: Escaping Putin's Russia: The father and daughter persecuted over a drawing
description: Alexei Moskalyov and his daughter, Maria, are symbolic victims of the Kremlin's repression. The father was jailed after posting an anti-war drawing his teenage daughter made at school. They have just arrived in France and are under humanitarian protection.

headline: Volodymyr Zelensky's exclusive interview with Le Monde on the war in Ukraine
description: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president shares his perspective on a possible end to the conflict and urges the US and all allied nations to maintain their support for Ukraine.

headline: Iranians are caught in the trap of Trump's incoherence
'Trump's vociferations no longer reassure financial markets'
Ukrainians' suffering must not be forgotten amid the war against Iran
